food_subgroup: Cooked vegetable dish with glaze and seasoning

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for maple-glazed cauliflower with shichimi and puffed rice. Closest parent is cauliflower food product (FOODON_03301710).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC represents plain cauliflower. No specific code exists for maple-glazed cauliflower with shichimi seasoning and puffed rice. Used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this prepared vegetable dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of maple glazed cauliflower is approximately 200 g, comprising roasted cauliflower florets coated in a shichimi-spiced maple glaze, served with curry seasoning and puffed rice as garnish.

nutrient_source.title: Cauliflower, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 169986); supplemented with maple syrup (FDC ID 169661) and shichimi togarashi component estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for maple glazed cauliflower with shichimi and puffed rice in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by component weighting: approximately 70% cooked cauliflower (USDA FDC 169986), 20% maple syrup contribution (USDA FDC 169661), and 10% puffed rice and shichimi seasoning (USDA FDC 168917 for puffed rice). Energy and macros reflect the combined glaze effect. Values are moderate-confidence estimates.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; maple syrup GI approximately 54; cauliflower GI low (<15); combined dish estimated at 55 reflecting maple syrup glaze dominance in glycemic contribution
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for maple glazed cauliflower with shichimi and puffed rice. GI estimated at 55 based on: cauliflower GI <15 (University of Sydney GI Database), maple syrup GI 54 (Atkinson et al. 2021, Am J Clin Nutr), and puffed rice GI ~82 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by approximate carbohydrate contribution per serving: cauliflower ~15%, maple syrup ~55%, puffed rice ~30%. Composite GI estimated at 55, classified as low per the ≤55 threshold. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (55 × 20.5 × 2.0) / 100 = 22.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from shichimi seasoning and glaze preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that this dish contains approximately 180 mg sodium per 100 g (360 mg per standard 200 g serving), which contributes meaningfully toward the 1500–2000 mg daily sodium limit recommended for hypertensive patients; portion control and requesting reduced-sodium preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Maple syrup glaze contributes significant free sugars and raises glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should note that the maple syrup glaze contributes approximately 12 g of sugar per 100 g and a glycemic load of approximately 22.6 per standard serving, which is moderate-to-high; pairing with a protein source and limiting portion size to 100–150 g can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and cholesterol-free profile; cauliflower provides beneficial phytonutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is low in saturated fat (0.2 g per 100 g) and contains no cholesterol, making it broadly compatible with a heart-healthy dietary pattern, though the moderate sodium content warrants attention for individuals managing blood pressure as a cardiovascular risk factor.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Maple Glazed Cauliflower is a Japanese-fusion vegetable dish served at To;asta restaurant in Singapore, featuring roasted cauliflower florets coated in a shichimi togarashi-spiced maple glaze, accompanied by curry seasoning and puffed rice for textural contrast. Shichimi togarashi is a traditional Japanese seven-spice blend typically containing red chili, sansho pepper, sesame seeds, dried citrus peel, ginger, nori, and hemp seeds, which imparts a complex heat and umami character to the sweet maple glaze. The dish is entirely plant-based, vegan, and dairy-free, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences. Nutritionally, it provides moderate energy at approximately 98 kcal per 100 g, with the majority of calories derived from carbohydrates (20.5 g per 100 g), primarily from the maple syrup glaze which contributes approximately 12 g of sugar per 100 g. Protein content is modest at 2.1 g per 100 g, and fat is low at 1.2 g per 100 g. Cauliflower is a good source of vitamin C (28 mg per 100 g), folate (44 mcg DFE per 100 g), and dietary fibre (2.0 g per 100 g), supporting immune function and digestive health. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low category), though the glycemic load per standard 200 g serving is approximately 22.6, which is moderate, primarily driven by the maple syrup glaze. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate portion size due to the free sugar content, while those managing hypertension should note the sodium contribution from the shichimi seasoning. The dish is well-suited as a vegetable side or light main course in a balanced meal.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in maple glazed cauliflower?
Is maple glazed cauliflower suitable for diabetics?
What is the glycemic index of maple glazed cauliflower?
What are the calories in maple glazed cauliflower?
Does maple glazed cauliflower contain dairy?
What restaurant serves maple glazed cauliflower in Singapore?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for maple glazed cauliflower with shichimi and puffed rice exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated by component weighting from USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked cauliflower, maple syrup, and puffed rice. GI is estimated from analogous foods in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Confidence is medium reflecting the estimation approach.
notes: Input record classified this dish under 'Grains and staples' and 'Japanese' cuisine with tags including 'Pasta' and 'Udon', which are inconsistent with the dish description. Food group has been corrected to 'Vegetables' and cuisine to 'Japanese-fusion' based on the dish composition (cauliflower, shichimi, maple syrup, puffed rice). Cuisine tags 'Pasta' and 'Udon' appear to be erroneous metadata from the source platform and have not been carried forward. The dish is served by one restaurant (To;asta - Circular Road) on GrabFood Singapore at SGD 12.00. Vitamin B12 is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based dish containing no animal products; confirmed zero by formulation. Vitamin D is 0.0 as cauliflower and plant-based ingredients contain negligible vitamin D; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Trans fat is 0.0 as no hydrogenated fats are used in this preparation; confirmed zero by formulation. Cholesterol is 0.0 as this is a vegan dish with no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero. Monounsaturated fat (-1 sentinel not used; estimated at 0.3 g), polyunsaturated fat (estimated at 0.4 g) are included as estimates from USDA cauliflower data. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not reliably reported for this estimated composite dish in the source database. GI set to 55 (low category, ≤55 threshold) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 as documented in gi_source. Glycemic load per 200 g serving calculated as (55 × 20.5 × 2.0) / 100 = 22.6 g.
